# OmniParse Study — Medical OCR Enhancement Analysis

> **Research Repository** — Study and analysis of [OmniParse](https://github.com/adithya-s-k/omniparse) for improving medical handwriting OCR.

---

## Repository Status

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| **Role** | Research / Study |
| **Status** | Study Complete |
| **Layer** | Study / Research |
| **Priority** | Low |
| **Related Fork** | [omniparse](https://github.com/DrAbdulmalek/omniparse) |

## What This Is

This repository contains **research notes, experiments, and analysis** of the OmniParse project, focused on how its parsing capabilities can enhance medical handwriting OCR accuracy and pipeline integration.

## Study Structure

```
omniparse-study/
├── notes/           # Analysis notes and observations
├── experiments/      # Benchmark experiments on medical samples
├── benchmarks/      # Performance comparison results
└── adoption-plan/    # Integration plan with medical OCR pipeline
```

## Key Findings

- OmniParse's multi-modal parsing approach is valuable for structured medical document extraction
- Integration with medical-ocr-postprocessor can improve end-to-end accuracy
- Arabic medical text requires additional customization beyond OmniParse's defaults

## Who Should Use This

- Researchers studying **multi-modal parsing for medical documents**
- Developers planning **OmniParse integration** with medical OCR systems
- Contributors looking to **extend** parsing capabilities for Arabic medical text

## When to Use This

| Need | Repository |

| Understand OmniParse study findings | **This repo** (omniparse-study) |
